## 2024-03-05 - [CSV Injection in Consortium Export]
**Vulnerability:** User-controlled data (consortium names, demographic labels, metrics) was not sanitized before being written to CSV exports in `apps/consortia/views.py`. The unsanitized `consortium.name` was also directly included in the `Content-Disposition` header filename.
**Learning:** This exposes the application to CSV injection (Formula execution in Excel/LibreOffice) and potential HTTP header injection/path traversal attacks in dynamically generated filenames. This pattern was missing in the newer consortia app despite protections existing in the older reports app.
**Prevention:** Always use `sanitise_csv_row` and `sanitise_filename` from `apps.reports.csv_utils` whenever dynamically generating CSV files and headers that contain user-provided text values.

## 2024-05-24 - [CRITICAL/HIGH] Fix HTTP Response Splitting / Path Traversal in Report Template Export
**Vulnerability:** In `apps/admin_settings/report_template_views.py`, the original CSV download endpoint constructed the `Content-Disposition` header filename by performing basic string replacement on the `profile.name` (`profile.name.replace(" ", "_").replace("/", "_")`). Since `profile.name` is user-controlled input, an attacker could include unhandled characters (like double quotes `"` and newline characters `\r\n`) in the name. This would allow them to perform HTTP Response Splitting, inject arbitrary HTTP headers, or escape the filename attribute to control the extension (e.g. executing an XSS payload upon download or altering the file's disposition type).
**Learning:** Even internal or admin-focused utility endpoints must rigorously sanitize filenames. Basic string replacement (like space-to-underscore) is insufficient protection against character injection in HTTP headers.
**Prevention:** Always use the established `sanitise_filename()` utility from `apps.reports.csv_utils` which enforces a strict allowlist (e.g., `[A-Za-z0-9_.-]`) when interpolating dynamic data into the `Content-Disposition` header.

## 2026-03-08 - [MEDIUM] Add FileExtensionValidator to CSV upload forms
**Vulnerability:** In `apps/surveys/forms.py` and `apps/plans/forms.py`, the `csv_file` field in `CSVImportForm` and `MetricImportForm` lacked explicit file extension validation, only checking `name.endswith(".csv")` inside the `clean_csv_file` method. This finding is M2 from the security review.
**Learning:** While the file extension was checked in the `clean_csv_file` method, relying solely on string manipulation rather than Django's built-in validation mechanisms could potentially be bypassed or lead to inconsistent validation across forms.
**Prevention:** Always use Django's built-in `FileExtensionValidator` for `FileField`s to enforce allowed extensions consistently and securely at the form field level.
